information: Book flights 6-8 weeks in advance for best prices. Use budget airlines and be flexible with dates. Avoid peak tourist seasons.
Pack light with versatile clothing. Bring a power bank, charger adapter, and copies of important documents. Use packing cubes for organization.
Always get travel insurance covering medical emergencies, trip cancellations, and lost luggage. Read the policy carefully for exclusions.
May-June and September-October offer pleasant weather, fewer crowds, and moderate prices. Summer is peak season with high costs and crowds.
Stay in well-reviewed accommodations. Share your itinerary with family. Keep valuables secure. Trust your instincts and avoid isolated areas at night.
Start early with main attractions. Use public transport or walking. Book skip-the-line tickets. Mix famous sites with local neighborhoods. Plan 2-3 main activities maximum.
Eat where locals eat, away from tourist areas. Try street food from busy stalls. Ask locals for recommendations. Take food tours to learn about the cuisine.
Adjust your sleep schedule a few days before travel. Stay hydrated during the flight. Get sunlight exposure upon arrival. Avoid long naps on the first day.
Shoot during golden hour at sunrise and sunset. Include people for scale. Explore beyond main attractions. Respect local customs when photographing people.
Notify your bank before travel. Use ATMs for best exchange rates. Carry a mix of cash and cards. Keep emergency cash separate. Avoid airport exchange kiosks.
